theme: Compress a string by committing replacement rules
title: Deadhand Grammar: Compress a String by Committing Replacement Rules
strategies: trivial: emit zero rules; grammar = raw axiom, size n (baseline 0.1)
greedy (RePair-style): repeatedly commit to the most frequent adjacent token pair while direct savings stay positive, no lookahead, pairs only

strong: candidate patterns of ALL lengths 2..min(L,8) scored by direct savings PLUS the best savings any other top candidate retains after the replacement is applied (1-step lookahead over overlap-destruction); commits whole-period rules and dodges decoy pairs
lookahead/dynamic-programming hybrids, exchange arguments over rule order, and residual-potential scoring remain open above the reference
